skill.name: python-executor
skill.description: Execute Python code in a safe sandboxed environment via inference.sh. Includes 100+ pre-installed libraries for data processing, web scraping, image manipulation, video creation, 3D model processing, PDF generation, API calls, and automation.
skill.summary: Run Python code in an isolated sandbox with 100+ libraries. Supports data analysis, web scraping, image processing, and automation tasks.
skill.author: inference-sh-skills

security_audit.summary: All 70 static findings are false positives. The backtick execution patterns are markdown code block delimiters and inline code formatting in a documentation file, not actual shell commands. Network URL findings are documentation links and example code snippets. The skill is a legitimate Python executor that runs code in a sandboxed environment via the belt CLI. No malicious intent or deceptive behavior was detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Misidentified as Shell Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: 58 static findings flagged backtick patterns as Ruby/shell command execution. These are actually markdown code block delimiters (```bash, ```json, ```python) and inline code formatting (`library_name`) in a documentation file. No actual shell execution occurs within the skill. All findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs and Example Code URLs Misidentified
low_findings.description: 7 findings flagged hardcoded URLs as network indicators. These are documentation links to inference.sh and example URLs (example.com, api.github.com) in code demonstration blocks. No malicious or suspicious destinations. All findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: Python HTTP Library Import in Example Code
low_findings.description: 2 findings flagged import requests in example code blocks. These are demonstration snippets showing how to use the executor for web scraping and API calls. The imports appear inside markdown code blocks and are instructional examples, not executable skill code. False positives.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm and Network Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: One finding claimed weak cryptographic algorithm at line 3 (YAML frontmatter description text) and one claimed network reconnaissance at line 165 (documentation text reading 'Safe execution - Runs in isolated subprocess'). Neither finding matches actual file content. Both are scan artifacts with no basis in the file.

content.user_title: Execute Python Code in a Sandboxed Environment
content.value_statement: Developers need a safe way to run Python code for data processing and automation without local setup. This skill executes Python scripts in a sandboxed environment with 100+ pre-installed libraries via the inference.sh platform.
actual_capabilities: Execute Python code in a sandboxed environment with 100+ pre-installed libraries
Process and visualize data using NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib
Perform web scraping with requests, BeautifulSoup, and browser automation tools
Manipulate images, create videos, and process 3D models programmatically
Call external APIs and generate PDF documents from Python scripts
Save output files that are automatically returned in the execution response
limitations: CPU-only environment with no GPU support for machine learning libraries
Maximum execution timeout of 300 seconds per script
Non-interactive execution requires file-based output for data retrieval
Internet access required for API calls and web scraping tasks

use_cases.title: Run Data Analysis Scripts
use_cases.description: Execute Python scripts for data processing, analysis, and visualization using Pandas, NumPy, and Matplotlib without any local setup.

use_cases.title: Scrape Web Content
use_cases.description: Extract data from websites using requests, BeautifulSoup, or browser automation tools like Selenium and Playwright in a sandboxed environment.

use_cases.title: Automate Python Workflows
use_cases.description: Run Python automation scripts for file processing, API integration, and document generation without managing local Python environments.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Simple Python Script
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this Python code and show me the output: print('Hello World')
prompt_templates.scenario: New user testing the Python executor for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Process Data with Pandas
prompt_templates.prompt: Load this sample data into a Pandas DataFrame and create a bar chart showing sales by name. Save the chart to the outputs directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Data analysis task with visualization output

prompt_templates.title: Scrape a Website
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the HTML content from a website using requests and parse it with BeautifulSoup to extract all the headings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Web scraping project for data collection

prompt_templates.title: Create a Video with MoviePy
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 3-second video with a blue background and centered white text using MoviePy. Set the resolution to 640x480 and save to the outputs directory.
prompt_templates.scenario: Video generation task for content creation

output_examples.input: Print the current date and time using Python
Create a bar chart from sample sales data using Matplotlib
output_examples.output: Chart saved to outputs/chart.png. The bar chart shows sales values for Alice (100) and Bob (150) with blue bars.

output_examples.input: Download a webpage and extract all image URLs using BeautifulSoup
output_examples.output: Found 12 images on the page. Extracted URLs include /images/logo.png, /photos/banner.jpg, and 10 more links.

best_practices: Set an appropriate timeout value for long-running operations to avoid execution failure
Save output files to the outputs directory for automatic retrieval in the response
Use plt.savefig() instead of plt.show() since the environment is non-interactive
anti_patterns: Writing infinite loops or recursion without setting a timeout limit
Attempting to use GPU-dependent libraries like TensorFlow or PyTorch in the CPU-only environment
Hardcoding API keys or credentials in Python scripts sent to the executor

faq.question: What Python version does the executor run?
faq.answer: The executor runs Python 3.10 in a sandboxed environment.

faq.question: Can I use machine learning libraries like TensorFlow?
faq.answer: No. The environment is CPU-only and does not include GPU-dependent machine learning libraries.

faq.question: How do I retrieve output files from my script?
faq.answer: Save files to the outputs directory. Files in this directory are automatically returned in the execution response.

faq.question: What is the maximum execution time for a script?
faq.answer: The maximum timeout is 300 seconds. The default is 30 seconds. You can configure this in the input.

faq.question: Is my code execution secure?
faq.answer: Yes. Each execution runs in an isolated sandboxed subprocess with no access to other users or the host system.

faq.question: Which libraries are pre-installed?
faq.answer: Over 100 libraries are pre-installed including NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, requests, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, Playwright, MoviePy, Pillow, OpenCV, and trimesh.
